article_title: Repeal of the 4,500-Litre Minimum Production Requirement for Wineries

briefs.title: BC Wineries No Longer Required to Produce 4,500 Litres Annually to Keep Their Licence
briefs.summary: BC has removed the rule that required wineries to produce at least 4,500 litres of wine each year to renew their licence. Wineries are now treated the same as breweries and distilleries — there is no minimum production target, though licences can still be flagged as dormant (inactive) if output is very low.
briefs.article_title: Repeal of the 4,500-Litre Minimum Production Requirement for Wineries
